title: Line Planner
description:
**Location:**

Ludvika, Dalarna County, Sweden
**Job ID:**

R0132598
**Date Posted:**

2026-06-22
**Company Name:**

HITACHI ENERGY SWEDEN AB
**Profession (Job Category):**

General Management
**Job Schedule:**

Full time
**Remote:**

No
**Job Description:**

**The Opportunity**

We are looking for a Line Planner control & Protection (Material and space) for HVDC to act as the center of expertise for planning and scheduling within the Line Planning organization. In this role, you will ensure that best execution practices are applied in tenders and project and will actively contribute to plan, monitor, and report these activities within your group. You will also provide operational support to the project planners and project managers within HVDC Control & Protection team on ad-hoc basis. This is a role with many interfaces and interesting work tasks within engineering and supply chain planning.


This role required operating with strict deadlines while conducting scenario-based analysis as a core part of daily responsibilities.


**How You’ll Make an Impact**

* The Line Planner Control & Protection (Material and space) is responsible to oversee availability of material and space on a macro level in order to ensure timely delivery of C&P scope within projects.
* Support line and project managers in planning methodology and on understanding and analyzing ordering, delivery and availability of key material and space for the Control and Protection.
* The Line Planner develops specific expertise in planning and execution of the line organizations scope in EPC HVDC deliveries.
* Establish accurate scheduling methods, best execution practices, and ensure that this is reflected in type schedules applied in project and tenders.
* Compiles information, analyses and makes recommendations in formats that assist effective management and decision making.
* Work with line managers to align material and space requirement and availability as per the scope of projects and tenders.
* Assist project managers in tenders and start of projects to ensure that best planning and execution practices are applied.
* Identify and mitigate bottlenecks, and risks to operational continuity from material and space aspect.
* Collaboration with various stakeholders involved in the process.

**Your background**

* You have a degree in Degree in Supply Chain, Engineering, or related field.
* Knowledge and 5+ years of experience from engineering / supply chain planning / test space planning, preferably with Energy sector.
* You are structured, flexible and hold some qualifications in project management.
* You have skills in planning and controlling a time schedule and have prior experience using Primavera P6 or MS Project.
* You have genuine technical interest and are always open to develop your skills within other fields.
* Knowledge how to use Microsoft's Office suite of software, especially Microsoft Excel, Word and PowerPoint.
* Fluent in written / spoken English, Swedish language skills would be an advantage.
* You are a dynamic and social person who enjoys working with people from various business areas and parts of world.

**What we offer**

* Collective agreement
* Flexible working time
* Health care and wellness allowance
* Fantastic career possibilities within Hitachi Energy both within Sweden and globally
* Mentor to support you throughout onboard phase
* Various trainings and education supporting employee development
* Diversified company with over 70+ nationalities working in Sweden
* Supplementary compensation for parental leave
Employee Benefit Portal with thousands of discounts and perks  
* 

**More about us**

A team with great co-operation and with diverse backgrounds is waiting for you to join! Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don’t delay – apply today!


Recruiting Manager Akshat Tyagi, akshat.tyagi@hitachienergy.com , will answer your questions about the position.
  

  

Union representatives - Sveriges Ingenjörer: Mikael Hjort, +46 107-38 25 73; Ledarna: Frank Hollstedt, +46 107-38 70 43; Unionen: Fredrik Holmgren, +46 107-38 21 85. All other questions can be directed to Talent Acquisition Specialist Antra Volujevica, antra.volujevica@hitachienergy.com


**Qualified individuals with a disability may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Hitachi Energy career site as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by completing a** **general inquiry form** **on our website. Please include your contact information and specific details about your required accommodation to support you during the job application process.**

title: Functional Safety Engineer F/M/X
description:
Job description
  
  

We're looking for an experienced Functional Safety Engineer to join our team in Gothenburg. In this role, you will contribute to the development of safety-critical automotive systems, ensuring compliance with functional safety standards throughout the product lifecycle. If you're passionate about system safety, risk assessment, and working in a collaborative international environment, we'd love to hear from you!
  
  

**Your Responsibilities:**  

Plan, perform, and document Hazard Analysis and Risk Assessments (HARA) in accordance with ISO 26262
  
Develop and maintain Functional Safety Concepts (FSC), Technical Safety Concepts (TSC), and safety cases for automotive systems
  
Define and refine safety requirements and safety mechanisms for safety-critical functions
  
Collaborate with system, hardware, software, testing, and supplier teams to ensure safety requirements are implemented effectively
  
Maintain traceability of safety requirements throughout the development lifecycle
  
Prepare, review, and manage functional safety work products, including safety plans, FMEA/FMEDA, and safety cases
  
Lead or support confirmation reviews and functional safety assessments
  
Support ISO 26262 audits by preparing evidence, conducting gap analyses, and following up on corrective actions
  
Drive safety-related discussions and contribute to continuous improvement of safety processes and methodologies
  
  

**Your Profile:**  

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
  
7 to 10 years of experience in functional safety engineering within the automotive industry
  
Strong practical experience with ISO 26262, including HARA, safety concepts, and safety case development
  
Experience preparing, reviewing, and maintaining functional safety work products
  
Knowledge of safety analysis techniques such as FMEA, FMEDA, FTA, SPFM, LFM, and PMHF
  
Experience supporting or participating in functional safety audits and assessments
  
Strong system engineering background in safety-critical automotive systems
  
Hands-on experience with requirements and safety management tools such as SystemWeaver, Medini, JIRA, DOORS, Codebeamer, Jama, XFMEA, or similar
  
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
  
Structured, analytical, and solution-oriented mindset with a strong sense of ownership
  
Professional proficiency in English
  
  

**What We Offer:**  

A truly international and multicultural work environment.
  
Access to training programs and certifications to accelerate your development.
  
An R&D lab where you can explore new technologies and approaches.
  
The opportunity to propose and lead innovative ideas within the company.
  
Personalized coaching and mentoring from experienced professionals.
  
A tailor-made career path, aligned with your growth ambitions.
  
  

Why Join Amaris Consulting?
  
  

**Agility:** Work in a dynamic, flexible, and stimulating environment.
  
**International exposure:** Collaborate with teams in over 60 countries and enjoy mobility opportunities.
  
**Intrapreneurship:** Take ownership of your ideas or create your own Business Unit.
  
**Supportive management:** Your manager will guide you based on your individual profile and goals.
  
  

**Salary range:** From 46 000 SEK to 52 000 SEK
  
  

The stated salary falls within the established salary range for the position and is determined based on experience, skills, and level of responsibility, in accordance with our policy of fair and non-discriminatory compensation.
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
  
  

Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!
company_description: Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. It gathers more than 7,500 people across 5 continents and more than 60 countries.

title: Software Lead Engineer
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a Software Lead Engineer to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
Scope of Role
  
The Software Lead Engineer / SW GDL is responsible for leading software-related activities for Door Control Functions and Door Control Units across SPA1/CMA, SPA2, and SPA3 platforms. The role drives supplier coordination, software delivery management, integration readiness, diagnostics, cybersecurity, release management, and software verification activities to ensure compliant and robust customer functionality.
  
The role acts as the technical software lead towards suppliers and internal stakeholders for all software-related development and release activities.
  
Required Qualifications
  
* Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Electrical, Electronics, Computer Science, Embedded Systems, or related engineering field.
* Minimum 8+ years of experience in Automotive Embedded Software Development and Integration.
* Strong understanding of automotive software lifecycle including requirements, design, implementation, integration, testing, and release management.
* Experience working with automotive communication protocols such as CAN, CANFD, LIN, Ethernet, and Diagnostics protocols.
* Good understanding of AUTOSAR concepts, embedded ECU software architecture, and automotive diagnostics.
* Experience in software verification, integration testing, HIL validation, and issue troubleshooting.
* Hands-on experience with automotive tools such as CANoe, vTestStudio, Lauterbach, CarWeaver, Jira, Confluence, Teamcenter, and Planisware.
* Experience in supplier management and SW joint reviews.
* Understanding of ISO 26262 Functional Safety and ISO 21434 Cybersecurity requirements.
* Experience in continuous integration, release management, and software delivery governance.
* Strong analytical, debugging, and communication skills.
* Previous experience working within Volvo Cars environment is preferred.


Key Responsibilities
  
* Lead software-related development and delivery activities for Door Control Functions and Door Control Units.
* Act as the primary SW technical interface towards ECU suppliers and internal stakeholders.
* Define, review, and maintain software requirements aligned with customer functions and system architecture.
* Coordinate supplier software deliveries, release plans, software maturity tracking, and integration readiness.
* Lead SW joint reviews, cybersecurity reviews, diagnostics reviews, and software quality assessments.
* Secure software integration readiness for HIL, bench, subsystem, and vehicle-level testing.
* Support development and maintenance of communication interfaces, signal mappings, and diagnostics implementations.
* Coordinate software verification activities including test planning, execution follow-up, and defect resolution.
* Drive Fault Report analysis, root cause investigations, and corrective actions through VIRA and related processes.
* Ensure software release documentation and delivery artifacts are completed according to VPDS and Volvo WoW.
* Support continuous improvement of test automation, software quality, and release efficiency.
* Collaborate with System Design, Hardware, Cybersecurity, Safety, and Vehicle Integration teams to ensure aligned implementation.
* Ensure software compliance with ISO 26262, ISO 21434, and Volvo Cars Standards.
* Support calibration planning and software configuration activities for different vehicle variants.
* Secure software-related data and documentation in systems such as PIE, CarWeaver, Teamcenter, and Planisware.


If you are passionate about bringing innovation to the projects you work on and want to join a global company, then this is the place for you.
  
**Tata Technologies:** Engineering a better world.
  
Tata Technologies would like to thank all applicants for their interest; each application will be reviewed against the set criteria for the role. We would like to advise that only candidates under consideration will be contacted. If you do not hear from us within 10 working days following the closing date, it will mean that unfortunately your application has not been successful. We will however retain your details for any suitable future opportunities.

title: Data Center Critical Facilities Engineer
description:
Job Summary
  
The Critical Facilities Engineer operates, manages, maintains, and repairs HVAC systems, critical and non‑critical power systems, plumbing, fire control and suppression systems, roofing, exterior grounds, and all related vendor-supported activities within a mission‑critical environment.
  
  

This fast‑paced role supports overall Data Center operations by maintaining and improving critical infrastructure through scheduled maintenance and ad hoc repairs and troubleshooting.
  
  

Leadership & Operational Excellence
  
Demonstrate strong technical leadership by building an engaged, confident team and maintaining clear operational oversight. Provide weekly management updates on preventive maintenance, corrective actions, risks, and upcoming work.
  
  

Proactively plan and lead engineering drills (Engineering Response, Bridge Communication, Environmental), review proactive vendor reports, and conduct regular team meetings to address challenges, lessons learned, and improvements.
  
  

Maintain full ownership of the change management process by submitting and reviewing change requests within the agreed customer notice period, ensuring timely approvals, thorough risk assessments, MOP creation and effective coordination with stakeholders and vendors to support safe, compliant, and resilient operations.
  
  

Responsibilities
  
Regular Infrastructure Maintenance
  
Completes all maintenance protocols, plans, etc. on time and accurately, including daily, weekly and monthly site checks
  
Performs all planned repairs to mechanical, electrical and special systems
  
Makes minor changes to mechanical, electrical and special systems if necessary
  
Troubleshoot and maintain office equipment if necessary, supporting auxiliary equipment and machines with problem solving and minor repairs to minimize downtime
  
Addresses, reports, resolves and investigates all major failures, significant problems, and delays
  
Ensures that sufficient spare parts are available on site for frequently used parts or parts with long lead times and maintains critical spares register
  
Monitors for and responds to BMS (Building Monitoring System) alarms in accordance with engineering policies and procedures
  
Maintains plumbing and fire suppression systems
  
  

Vendor Management
  
Coordinates with vendors to ensure that maintenance activities are carried out as per Equinix's requirements
  
Edits and tracks the work approval process for service providers (including briefing at the start of work, control of work during the day, final review of the work carried out)
  
Recommend changes/updates to technical files for plants and equipment in cooperation with vendor, and the supervisors, ensuring as-built are up to date
  
  

Process and Policy Administration
  
Creates engineering scripts (switching schedules)
  
Ensures that all Equinix safety standards are followed
  
Ensures that quality procedures related to maintenance are followed
  
Ensures correct Incident Management processes are followed
  
Reviews processes, procedures, systems. Communicates process deviations and proposed changes to a higher level so that relevant follow-up measures can be carried out
  
Develops training and supports training of other Data Center (International Business Exchange) employees in relevant engineering topics and processes
  
  

Qualifications
  
Knowledge and Experience
  
Understanding of relevant codes, compliance regulations, and applicable safe work requirements
  
Familiar with common documents as it applies to design, documents, schemes, data sheets, and technical information
  
  

**Relevant experience may include:**  

Building (Facility) Maintenance with experience working in large commercial properties such as a high rise building or a convention center
  
Maintenance technicians working in critical environments such as a hospital, hotel, or manufacturing plant where it’s critical if you lose power or the AC or heating unit goes down
  
General mechanical experience such as Generator Repair, Auto Mechanic, Aircraft Mechanic, Electrician, HVAC Technician, etc.
  
Refrigeration handling certification preferred, electrical/mechanical license preferred
  
  

Skills and Attributes
  
  

**Essential Duties/Physical specifications include:**  

Able to perform all essential job functions, including walking, standing, bending, stooping, climbing, lifting and manual dexterity, with or without reasonable accommodation
  
Ability to work days/nights/weekends/holidays, if needed and/or required
  
Familiar with all common applications needed to perform daily tasks, including CMMS, CRMs, productivity software, and draft drawing softwares
  
Skilled at working in alignment with all relevant safety measures
  
Skilled at communicating with and managing vendors
  
Able to communicate effectively with others, including oral and written instructions and reporting
  
Excellent problem solving and teamwork skills
  
  

Equinix is committed to ensuring that our employment process is open to all individuals, including those with a disability. If you are a qualified candidate and need assistance or an accommodation, please let us know by completing this form.
  
  

Equinix is an Equal Employment Opportunity and, in the U.S., an Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to unlawful consideration of race, color, religion, creed, national or ethnic origin, ancestry, place of birth, citizenship, sex, pregnancy / childbirth or related medical conditions, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or domestic partnership status, age, veteran or military status, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, political / organizational affiliation, status as a victim or family member of a victim of crime or abuse, or any other status protected by applicable law.

title: Technical Lead
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a Technical Lead to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
The role
  
We are seeking an experienced Vehicle Test Engineer to execute end-to-end Validation & Verification (V&V) of Door Control Functions at vehicle, subsystem, and component levels.
  
The role focuses on ensuring customer function compliance, early issue detection, and supporting successful integration and release in line with automotive development milestones.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
Vehicle Testing & Validation
  
**Perform vehicle-level testing (VIL) for:** Door Lock/Unlock functions
  
Side Door Manoeuvring (open/close, anti-pinch, safety features)
  
Window operation (local & global open/close)
  
Controls Disable & Protected Interior Storage
  
Validate functions against customer requirements across all test levels (vehicle, subsystem, component)
  
  

Test Execution & Coverage
  
Develop, maintain, and execute test cases
  
Work with automation to understand the process, later stage you must maintain and update automation scripts
  
Ensure requirement traceability and test coverage across CF, PF, PC, SM levels
  
Support regression and integration testing for software releases
  
  

Fault Detection & Analysis
  
Identify, reproduce, and document issues observed during testing
  
Perform log analysis (CAN/LIN/Ethernet signals) and basic debugging
  
Raise ad track defects in VIRA/Jira
  
Collaborate with SW/HW teams for root cause analysis
  
  

Integration & Release Support
  
Support software flashing, vehicle integration, calibration, and release validation
  
Participate in build verification, integration testing, and gate activities
  
Ensure readiness for vehicle builds and software releases
  
  

Required Qualifications
  
Bachelor’s degree in Electronics / Electrical / Automotive Engineering
  
Swedish Valid driving license (mandatory for vehicle testing)
  
  

**5–10 years of experience in:**  

Vehicle / System / ECU Testing
  
Door / Body / Closure domain testing (preferred)
  
  

Technical Skills
  
Strong knowledge of vehicle electrical systems and door control functions
  
Vector tools (CANoe, CANalyzer)
  
Diagnostics protocols (UDS)
  
ECU flashing and signal/log analysis
  
Familiarity with HIL/VIL testing and test automation using vTestStudio
  
Understanding of OEM process and automotive V-model development
  
  

Key Competencies
  
Strong analytical and debugging skills
  
High attention to detail and quality
  
Effective communication and teamwork
  
Proactive and hands-on approach
  
Ability to work in cross-functional and multi-platform environments
  
  

If you are passionate about bringing innovation to the projects you work on and want to join a global company, then this is the place for you.
  
**Tata Technologies:** Engineering a better world.

We would like to advise that only candidates under consideration will be contacted. If you do not hear from us within 10 working days following the closing date, it will mean that unfortunately your application has not been successful. We will however retain your details for any suitable future opportunities.

title: Diagnostics Engineer
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a Diagnostics Engineer to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
The role
  
We are seeking a skilled Fault Tracer, responsible for first-hand fault investigation, troubleshooting, and technical coordination. This role focuses on identifying, classifying, and resolving faults across multiple environments such as Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) setups and vehicle systems.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
Perform first-line fault tracing and troubleshooting in HIL and vehicle environments.
  
Analyze issues and classify root causes (e.g., hardware, software, or system/setup-related).
  
Generate detailed fault reports with logs, findings, and technical insights.
  
Utilize debugging tools to capture and analyze system behavior and logs.
  
Support software download and flashing activities on Devices Under Test (DUT) using debuggers.
  
Coordinate with cross-functional teams to ensure timely resolution of faults.
  
  

Required Qualifications
  
Strong knowledge of CAN communication and UDS (Unified Diagnostic Services).
  
Good understanding of inverter systems in automotive applications.
  
Hands-on experience with debugging tools, especially Lauterbach Trace32.
  
Proven experience in testing and troubleshooting embedded systems.
  
Proficiency with Vector tools such as CANoe and CANalyzer.
  
  

Preferred (Meritorious) Skills
  
Experience in the automotive industry, preferably with OEMs such as Volvo Cars.
  
Familiarity working with Tier 1 and Tier 2 suppliers.
  
Swedish Driving License – B, is added Plus
  
  

If you are passionate about bringing innovation to the projects you work on and want to join a global company, then this is the place for you.
  
**Tata Technologies:** Engineering a better world.
  
Tata Technologies would like to thank all applicants for their interest; each application will be reviewed against the set criteria for the role. We would like to advise that only candidates under consideration will be contacted. If you do not hear from us within 10 working days following the closing date, it will mean that unfortunately your application has not been successful. We will however retain your details for any suitable future opportunities.

title: Technical Lead
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a Technical Lead to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
Role Overview
  
We are seeking a Technical Lead / Software Test Automation Engineer. The role focuses on test automation development, framework enhancement, and integration support in collaboration with infrastructure and testing teams.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
Develop, maintain, and enhance test automation frameworks driven by infrastructure and testing requirements.
  
Design and implement automated test scripts using Python and CAPL (Vector toolchain).
  
Translate test specifications from testing teams into robust automated test cases.
  
Collaborate with infrastructure teams on framework refactoring and scalability improvements.
  
Manage code using GIT and Gerrit, ensuring version control and traceability.
  
Integrate and execute test automation within CI pipelines (Zuul, Cynosure).
  
Handle ambiguous requirements through proactive communication and cross-team alignment.
  
Contribute to continuous improvements in automation quality, performance, and maintainability.
  
  

Required Skills & Competencies
  
Technical Skills
  
Strong programming skills in Python.
  
Experience with CAPL / Vector tools (e.g., CANoe).
  
Hands-on experience in test automation and framework development.
  
Experience with version control systems (GIT, Gerrit).
  
Knowledge of CI/CD tools (Zuul, Cynosure or similar).
  
  

Soft Skills
  
Strong communication and collaboration skills.
  
Ability to work in cross-functional teams.
  
Proactive and adaptable mindset for dynamic environments.
  
Focus on quality and continuous improvement.
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
Experience in automotive or embedded systems domain.
  
Familiarity with software integration and verification workflows.
  
  

If you are passionate about bringing innovation to the projects you work on and want to join a global company, then this is the place for you.
  
**Tata Technologies:** Engineering a better world.

We would like to advise that only candidates under consideration will be contacted. If you do not hear from us within 10 working days following the closing date, it will mean that unfortunately your application has not been successful. We will however retain your details for any suitable future opportunities.

title: Software Lead Engineer
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a Software Lead Engineer to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
  

**Key Responsibilities:**  

Lead and coordinate complete software development activities for the Steering Gear Module.
  
Set up and maintain project and delivery plans, align stakeholders, and manage deliverables and deadlines.
  
Drive deployment of software to the manufacturing plant and aftermarket channels.
  
Handle effective communication and coordination across internal teams, project partners, and suppliers.
  
Act as the main point of contact and representative for the complete software stack.
  
Ensure end-to-end alignment of development flows, interfaces, and project visibility.
  
Analyse aftermarket data and fault reports to assess severity, identify root causes, and implement corrective actions.
  
Identify risks, prepare mitigation plans, and ensure clear escalation paths.
  
Maintain an always-updated delivery roadmap, including sub-function and requirement-level progress.
  
Conduct software checks and validation prior to delivery to customers.
  
Organize and lead cross-functional events and coordination meetings involving multiple stakeholders.
  
Support integration activities from a vehicle program perspective, ensuring timelines and technical compatibility.
  
  

**Tool & System Proficiency:**  

**Development & Tracking Tools:** VIRA, Lotus Notes, ConfigHub, PIE R&D
  
**Diagnostics & Communication Tools:** Carcom, Elektra, Canoe, Canalyzer, KDP
  
**Protocols & Standards:** UDS 14229, OBDONUDS
  
**Testing & Analysis:** HIL testing, log analysis, fault report interpretation
  
  

**Required Qualifications:**  

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical, Electronics, Computer Science, or a related engineering field.
  
8+ years of experience in embedded software delivery or automotive electronics.
  
Strong understanding of software lifecycle, diagnostic protocols, and control systems.
  
Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams and coordinate complex delivery scenarios.
  
Analytical skills with experience interpreting aftermarket data and fault trends.
  
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  
Proactive mindset with a solution-oriented approach to delivery and risk management.
  
  

If you are passionate about bringing innovation to the projects you work on and want to join a global company, then this is the place for you.

title: HIL Systems Engineer
description:
With more than 54 locations throughout Europe, the Bertrandt Group has been providing engineering and development solutions for the international automotive and aerospace industries since 1974. More than 12,000 employees use their know-how and innovative strength every day to support our customers – leading manufacturers and OEMs – with integrated solutions in management, design, and industrialization.
  
Bertrandt is now expanding into Sweden, establishing a strong and future-oriented presence in Gothenburg. Our activities cover a spectrum of expertise from electrical, electronics, and software development to design engineering, project management, quality, industrialization, and validation, all driven by innovation and a passion for technology.
  
  

**What to expect:**  

Coordinates the integration of Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) systems, ensuring efficient setup and functionality across development stages.
  
Maintain and optimize test benches and automation environments to support validation activities.
  
Coordinate global teams for software and hardware integration and testing.
  
Support the development of validation setups using MATLAB/Simulink, dSPACE, Python, and related tools.
  
Collaborate closely with system engineers, software developers, and validation teams to ensure seamless workflow and data consistency.
  
  

**What you bring along:**  

Experience with HIL modeling and automation environments within automotive or embedded systems.
  
Knowledge of MATLAB/Simulink, dSPACE, Automotive Ethernet, CAN, and Python.
  
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines, software verification, and test automation frameworks.
  
Fluent English (spoken and written).
  
Structured, proactive, and analytical working style.
  
This role requires on-site collaboration at our Gothenburg location and candidates must hold valid work authorization in Sweden.

title: HIL Engineer
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a HIL Engineer to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
  

**Role Overview:**  

As a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) Engineer, you will join a collaborative team focused on designing, developing, and maintaining electrical HIL rigs at both component and domain levels. These rigs enable early-stage integration and verification of hardware and software by simulating real vehicle environments using advanced Vector tools. Your work ensures that HIL systems evolve to support emerging technologies such as electrification and autonomous driving.
  
You will contribute to continuous improvements in test automation, simulation models, and integration strategies, gaining broad exposure to vehicle systems and electrical architectures while driving innovation in automated testing and continuous integration.
  
  

**Key Responsibilities:**  

Lead the design, development, and maintenance of HIL rigs.
  
Drive integration efforts across multiple product domains.
  
Commission and validate HIL test objects.
  
Diagnose and resolve hardware issues, including harnesses and simulations.
  
Manage ECU updates and component configurations on HIL rigs.
  
Develop network simulations (Gateways, signal manipulation, etc.).
  
Support manual software downloads for core ECUs.
  
  

**Profile:**  

You are a problem-solver with a collaborative mindset, capable of managing multiple tasks in parallel. You value quality, continuous improvement, and innovative approaches to processes and methods.
  
  

**Qualifications:**  

Master’s or Bachelor’s degree in Electronics, Mechatronics, Physics, or similar.
  
5+ years of relevant experience.
  
  

**Skills & Expertise:**  

Strong background in electronic systems development (HW & SW).
  
Hands-on experience working with Core or Central computing cluster ECUs like VCUs, Zonal Architecture ECUs.
  
Hands-on experience with HIL and automated test systems using Vector tools.
  
Proficiency in Python and CAPL for scripting and automation.
  
Deep understanding of automotive communication protocols (CAN, LIN, Ethernet).
  
Skilled in CANoe for simulation and data analysis.
  
  

**Familiarity with Vector hardware components:**  

VT System modules (VT RT Modules, VT cards)
  
RT Rack and compatible I/O boards
  
Vector Network Interface cards
  
Power supply units and control systems
  
LINUX environment, knowledge of managing folder structure and install /update OS
  
Flashing software – SW verification and fault tracing
  
Bringing up ECU boards with firmware
  
  

**Tools:**  

CANoe, vTESTstudio
  
Vector VT Systems
  
Vector VT RT Modules
  
Vector RT Racks
  
Python (C# is a plus)

title: System Test Engineer Automotive
description:
About the role
  
The System Test Engineer ensures system quality and maturity through structured verification and validation activities across automotive projects. The role focuses on system-level testing and close collaboration with development and integration teams.
  
This is a general role suitable for various automotive system test assignments.
  
With more than 54 locations throughout Europe, the Bertrandt Group has been providing engineering and development solutions for the international automotive and aerospace industries since 1974. More than 12,000 employees use their know-how and innovative strength every day to support our customers – leading manufacturers and OEMs – with integrated solutions in management, design, and industrialization.
  
Bertrandt is now expanding into Sweden, establishing a strong and future-oriented presence in Gothenburg. Our activities cover a spectrum of expertise from electrical, electronics, and software development to design engineering, project management, quality, industrialization, and validation, all driven by innovation and a passion for technology.
  
  

**What to expect:**  

Plan, execute, and document system-level test activities
  
Perform verification and validation according to project requirements
  
Analyze test results and report deviations
  
Collaborate closely with development and integration teams
  
Support continuous improvement of test processes and methods
  
Contribute to quality assurance across development phases
  
  

**What you bring along:**  

Experience as a System Test Engineer within automotive
  
Knowledge of verification and validation processes
  
Experience with test environments and tools
  
Analytical and quality-focused mindset
  
Structured working style and good communication skills
  
Fluent in English and Swedish
  
Valid work permit for Sweden

title: Software Test Engineer
description:
At Tata Technologies we make product development dreams a reality by designing, engineering and validating the products of tomorrow for the world’s leading manufacturers. Due to our continued growth, we are now recruiting for a Software Test Engineer to strengthen our team in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
The role
  
We are looking for SW Test & Integration Engineers to support complete‑vehicle testing in collaboration with CSIR test teams and VIL teams. The role focuses on exploratory and automated testing, system integration, and ensuring robustness of the full electrical architecture across multiple domains.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
Develop and execute comprehensive test cases across integration layers and user journeys, covering the full electrical architecture.
  
Continuously implement new test scenarios based on insights, findings, and evolving product requirements—both domain‑specific and complete‑vehicle robustness testing.
  
Take operational responsibility for the end‑to‑end test flow, including monitoring execution, maintaining stable test suites, and ensuring high‑quality data output in collaboration with rig/HW teams.
  
Perform exploratory, automated, and regression testing to identify issues before customer exposure.
  
Conduct fault analysis and reporting, collaborating with product streams for timely resolution and quality improvements.
  
Support integration of new features/functions into VIL and rig setups; troubleshoot issues across SW, HW, and communication networks.
  
  

Required Skills & Experience
  
Hands-on experience in automotive testing—preferably in VIL, SIL/HIL, or complete‑vehicle environments.
  
Strong ability to analyze complete vehicle network logs including CAN, LIN, and Ethernet.
  
Good knowledge and practical experience with Vector CANoe and Vector VT Systems.
  
Proficiency in Python, CAPL, or similar scripting tools for test development and automation, including working with shared repos and common libraries.
  
Understanding of vehicle domains such as high-voltage systems, BEV propulsion, thermal systems, and safety systems.
  
Valid Swedish Driving License – Category B.
  
Ability to work with evolving requirements and collaborate effectively across teams.
  
  

Soft Skills
  
Strong analytical and troubleshooting mindset.
  
Ability to operate efficiently with vague or changing prerequisites.
  
Clear communication, structured documentation, and strong teamwork skills.

title: Fullstack Engineer — Financial Engineering, Creator Monetization
description:
We are looking for a
 **Fullstack Engineer** 
 to join the Creator Monetization squad within Financial Engineering. Our mission is to ensure that creators are getting paid accurately and on time — a critical part of enabling more creators to live off their art. As we rapidly scale our creator programs across audiobooks and podcasts, our systems will be processing higher volumes of financial data powering payouts for creators worldwide. This work is highly visible across the company, touching
 **Finance** 
 ,
 **Commerce** 
 , and the
 **Business Verticals** 
 . You'll work across the full stack — from creator- and finance-facing UIs to backend services and internal tooling — and gain exposure to a wide range of business domains along the way.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Design, build, and maintain fullstack features across backend services and internal as well as external-facing UIs that power creator monetization workflows
* Work across the stack using Typescript and React, Java, PostgreSQL, and GCP to deliver end-to-end solutions
* Contribute to the reliability and scalability of financial data pipelines and payments infrastructure
* Build and improve internal tooling and back-office systems used by finance and operations teams
* Collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, and cross-functional partners across Finance, Commerce, and Operations
* Use AI-assisted development tools to move faster while maintaining accountability for quality and outcomes
* Continuously improve monitoring, testing, and operational practices across production systems


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience building and operating fullstack applications in production environments
* You are proficient in Java and have experience with modern frontend development (TypeScript and React)
* You have practical experience with relational databases such as PostgreSQL
* You are comfortable working across frontend and backend systems and care about how they connect
* You have a solid understanding of APIs, system design, and modern web architecture
* You take ownership of what you ship, from implementation through production health
* You are comfortable using AI-assisted development tools and take responsibility for validating outputs
* You communicate clearly and collaborate effectively with engineers and cross-functional partners
* You care about writing clean, maintainable code that others can easily understand and extend
* You are curious, proactive, and motivated to learn through experimentation and feedback
* Experience with financial systems, payment platforms, or high-volume data processing is a plus


**Start:** 
 July/August
   

  

**End:** 
 2026-01 or 02
   

  

**Workplace:** 
 Sweden.
